items.title: Singular Value Decomposition
items.description: PCA on centered data is computed via SVD of X̃; right singular vectors are principal directions and singular values rank explained variance.

items.title: Eigenvalues and eigenvectors
items.description: Principal components are eigenvectors of the covariance matrix ordered by eigenvalue magnitude; see that topic for the eigenvalue equation and symmetric-matrix properties.

items.title: Covariance and correlation
items.description: PCA eigendecomposes the sample covariance matrix—or the correlation matrix after standardizing features; preprocessing choice determines the geometry.

items.title: Curse of dimensionality
items.description: High p with limited n makes distances sparse and models harder; PCA is a classic linear response that compresses features before modeling or visualization.

items.title: Embedding visualization
items.description: First two or three PCA components are often plotted for EDA; non-linear embedding methods may reveal structure PCA misses when manifolds are curved.

items.title: Scaling and normalization
items.description: Standardizing features before PCA switches from covariance-based to correlation-based principal directions.
